description: Test cases for the comment-review composition skill. Each eval exercises the 3-protocol pipeline (/inquire × /gap × /contextualize + channel-loop) against a different artifact type + fixation event combination, verifying the caller-supplied signature (artifact_path, fixation_event D, application_context) routes scopes correctly and that the channel-loop augmentation activates for the terminal applicability check.

evals.name: blog-post-publish
evals.prompt: I've got a draft at ~/.claude/.write/2026-04-21-epistemic-protocols-composition.md for a Medium post about how epistemic protocols handle composition — I cite Ray Amjad's artifact framework and reference our own graph.json structure. Before I publish to medium.com and linkedin.com I want a full review: verify my factual claims (especially the Ray Amjad attribution and a couple of stats about LLM gate-response rates I pulled from a paper), audit for consideration gaps at the publish decision (counter-arguments, audience misfit, disclosure of AI-assisted writing), and then render it in a browser so I can leave inline comments on specific sentences. Walk me through it.
evals.expected_output: Runs the three protocols in pipeline order with D='publish' (stakes High — externally visible). Phase 1: /inquire dispatches to CanonicalExternal for Ray Amjad source + paper cites, UserTacit for stats that lack web source. Phase 2: /gap surfaces consideration/audience/disclosure gaps. Phase 3: /contextualize + channel-loop — bun scripts/serve.ts launches, browser opens for inline comments on the rendered preview. feedback-2026-04-21-epistemic-protocols-composition.jsonl accumulates comments for re-entry.

evals.name: plan-file-commit
evals.prompt: Before I start executing on ~/.claude/plans/anamnesis-stage3-hypomnesis-write.md I need a thorough review. The plan references current Anamnesis Phase 0 scan behavior, cites the graph.json advisory edges from anamnesis to 9 downstream protocols, and proposes a new SessionEnd hook schedule. I want to verify the factual references are still accurate (the protocol may have shifted since I drafted this), audit planning gaps (what could I be missing about rollback, what alternative implementation paths I haven't considered, what assumptions I'm carrying silently), and check that the plan actually fits the current worktree + merge-freeze context. Then I want to leave inline notes in the browser before I commit to executing any of this.
evals.expected_output: Runs with D='commit to execution', application_context='current worktree + merge freeze state'. Phase 1: /inquire verifies graph.json edges via CodeDerivable (read .claude/skills/verify/graph.json), verifies Anamnesis Phase 0 behavior via CodeDerivable (read anamnesis SKILL.md). Phase 2: /gap surfaces procedural/alternative/assumption gaps at the commit-to-execution decision with default stakes High (irreversible start of execution). Phase 3: /contextualize scans Convention/Environment/Dependency against the plan, channel-loop opens for inline plan notes.

evals.name: realign-direction-line
evals.prompt: I just ran /realign and the fused direction line plus per-horizon trace is staged in CLAUDE.md as a working diff. Before I commit and push the new line to main I want a full review: factual references in the trace (cited issue numbers, PR review excerpts, closed disposition trajectory entries) should match what's actually in the repository, consideration gaps at the direction-line commit (what if accumulated open issues since the trace shift the fusion the moment after I push? what rollback failure modes am I not anticipating?), and whether the fused line actually fits the categorization-axis use that downstream skills like /triage make of the project guide direction. I need to catch drift before it pollutes the next session's first-utterance baseline.
evals.expected_output: Runs with D='commit and push direction-line update', application_context='fresh-context next session reading the inscribed line as northstar'. Phase 1: /inquire verifies factual claims via CodeDerivable (issue numbers via gh, PR review quotes via gh) + git log for closed-disposition trajectory consistency. Phase 2: /gap surfaces assumption/alternative gaps at the commit decision with High stakes (the line is read by every fresh session). Phase 3: /contextualize scans for Convention (CLAUDE.md direction-line format) / Environment (open-issue landscape vs trace timestamp) / Dependency (downstream skills consuming the line as a categorization axis) mismatches; channel-loop opens for inline annotations.
